INGREDIENTS:
<ul>
<li>fresh lime juice (1 oz)</li>
<li>rock candy syrup (0.25 oz)</li>
<li>orgeat (0.25 oz)</li>
<li>orange curacao (0.5 oz)</li>
<li>aged Martinique agricole (1 oz)</li>
<li>aged pot-still Jamaican rum (1 oz)</li>
<li>crushed ice (1 cup)</li>
<li>lime shell (1)</li>
<li>mint sprig (1)</li>
</ul>

INSTRUCTIONS:
<aside class="callout callout-note" role="note"><span>Vic's 1944 spec called for a single 17-year J. Wray & Nephew Jamaican rum; modern reconstructions split the base between aged pot-still Jamaican and aged Martinique agricole to approximate the lost rum's profile. The Mai Tai's structural bones are the Jamaican planter's-punch sour tradition (one of sour, two of sweet, three of strong) — it descends from West Indian punch, not Polynesian invention.</span></aside>
<aside class="callout callout-note" role="note"><span>Brand recommendations. Best: Smith & Cross or Hamilton Pot Still Black for the Jamaican; Rhum J.M. VSOP or Clément VSOP for the agricole; Pierre Ferrand Dry Curaçao; Small Hand Foods or BG Reynolds orgeat. Economical: Appleton Estate 8 + Plantation 3 Stars (or Denizen Merchant's Reserve as a single-bottle stand-in); Cointreau; Liber & Co. orgeat. Single-bottle alternative: Denizen Merchant's Reserve, an 8-year blend of Plummer-style Jamaican pot-still rum and Martinique rhum grand arôme. It was developed by Nick Pelis with Martin Cate of Smuggler's Cove specifically to fill the slot of the lost J. Wray & Nephew 17, approximating the blend Vic himself reached for in the "second adjusted" Mai Tai recipe after the original Jamaican rum stock dried up. Pour 2 oz of Merchant's Reserve in place of the split rum bill.</span></aside>
<ol>
<li>In a <span class="cookware">shaker</span> combine <span class="ingredient" data-amount="1" data-unit="oz">fresh lime juice</span>, <span class="ingredient" data-amount="0.25" data-unit="oz">rock candy syrup</span>, <span class="ingredient" data-amount="0.25" data-unit="oz">orgeat</span>, <span class="ingredient" data-amount="0.5" data-unit="oz">orange curacao</span>, <span class="ingredient" data-amount="1" data-unit="oz">aged Martinique agricole</span>, and <span class="ingredient" data-amount="1" data-unit="oz">aged pot-still Jamaican rum</span>.</li>
<li>Add <span class="ingredient" data-amount="1" data-unit="cup">crushed ice</span> and shake until chilled, about <button type="button" class="timer" data-value="10" data-unit="seconds" aria-label="Start 10 seconds timer">10 seconds</button>.</li>
<li>Pour unstrained into a <span class="cookware">double old-fashioned glass</span>.</li>
<li>Garnish with the spent <span class="ingredient" data-amount="1" data-unit="">lime shell</span> and a <span class="ingredient" data-amount="1" data-unit="">mint sprig</span>.</li>
</ol>
